name: Dynamic Stretch Routine
desc: Full-body dynamic stretching sequence including leg swings, arm circles, hip rotations, and torso twists. Prepares joints and muscles for high-intensity football activity.

name: High Knees & Butt Kicks
desc: Players jog across the field alternating between high knees and butt kicks. Activates hip flexors and hamstrings while raising heart rate.

name: Lateral Shuffle Activation
desc: Players shuffle laterally across 10-yard lanes, focusing on low hips, wide base, and quick feet. Activates hip abductors and prepares for lateral movement.

name: Carioca Footwork
desc: Players perform carioca (grapevine) steps across the field, rotating hips over and under. Develops hip mobility and coordination essential for football movement patterns.

name: Backpedal & Break
desc: Players backpedal 10 yards then break hard at a 45-degree angle on coach's signal. Activates defensive back mechanics and prepares hips for change of direction.

name: Inchworm Push-Up Walk
desc: Players walk hands out to a push-up position, perform one push-up, walk feet to hands and repeat. Activates the full posterior chain and core.

name: Resistance Band Hip Activation
desc: Using bands around ankles, players perform monster walks, lateral steps, and clamshells. Activates glutes and hip stabilizers critical for explosion off the line.

name: Acceleration Build-Ups
desc: Players run 40 yards at progressive intensities — 50%, 70%, 90%, then full speed. Prepares the neuromuscular system for explosive sprinting.

name: A-Skip & B-Skip Mechanics
desc: Players perform A-skips (drive knee up, push off ground) and B-skips (extend leg forward after knee drive). Reinforces sprint mechanics and foot strike patterns.

name: Ball Handling Activation
desc: Players rotate the ball around their waist, through their legs, and in figure-8 patterns. Wakes up hand-eye coordination and ball security awareness before contact drills.

name: Partner Reaction Drill
desc: Partners face each other 2 yards apart. One player mirrors the other's movements on visual cue. Develops reaction time and first-step quickness.

name: Team Stretch Circle
desc: Full team stretches together in a circle led by a captain. Includes static quad, hamstring, groin, and shoulder stretches. Builds team cohesion and full-body readiness.

name: Stance & Start Fundamentals
desc: Players work their stance — two-point, three-point, and four-point — focusing on weight distribution, heel-to-toe balance, and first-step explosiveness off the line.

name: Footwork Ladder Sequence
desc: Agility ladder drills including single-leg hops, lateral ins-and-outs, and two-in two-out patterns. Develops foot speed and coordination for all positions.

name: Mirror Drill
desc: Two players face each other across a line. One leads, one mirrors. Develops reactive agility, lateral quickness, and hip flexibility required in man-coverage and run defense.

name: Cone Change-of-Direction
desc: 5-10-5 shuttle and L-cone drills timed individually. Measures and develops lateral quickness and the ability to decelerate and re-accelerate through direction changes.

name: Explosion Box Drill
desc: Four cones set in a square. Players sprint to each cone using different movements — forward sprint, lateral shuffle, backpedal, diagonal break. Full movement vocabulary in one drill.

name: Ball Security Gauntlet
desc: Ball carriers run through a gauntlet of coaches and players swatting at the ball. Reinforces four-point contact (fingertips, wrist, forearm, bicep) and ball protection under pressure.

name: One-on-One Competitive
desc: Individual match-ups across all positions — WR vs CB, TE vs LB, RB vs safety. Competitive reps that reveal individual technique and toughness. Film for position group review.

name: Hand Fighting Technique
desc: Offensive and defensive players practice hand placement, punch timing, and swim/rip moves in controlled partner situations. Critical for O-line/D-line and WR/CB match-ups.

name: Leverage & Pad Level
desc: Players work with sleds and shields on maintaining low pad level through contact. Emphasizes bending at the knees and driving through the point of attack.

name: Reaction Ball Drops
desc: Coach drops a reaction ball and players sprint to field it before the second bounce. Develops reaction time, first-step quickness, and ball tracking for all skill positions.

name: Route Tree Individual
desc: QBs throw to receivers running each route on the tree — out, in, curl, comeback, post, corner, go, slant, flat. Establishes route precision and QB-WR timing on all nine routes.

name: 7-on-7 vs Cover 2
desc: Offense runs scripted plays against a Cover 2 shell. Focus on identifying safety help, attacking the deep middle seam, and throwing the 5-yard out before the corner squeezes.

name: 7-on-7 vs Cover 3
desc: Offense attacks Cover 3 by targeting the curl-flat, attacking the boundary corner with fades, and hitting the deep third holes. QB reads single-high safety rotation.

name: 7-on-7 vs Man Coverage
desc: Offense runs routes against man coverage without a free safety. WRs work releases at the line of scrimmage. QBs throw back shoulder and trust the route-runner's separation.

name: Quick Game Install
desc: Focus on the slant-flat, hitch-seam, and stick-nod concepts. QB catches snap, sets feet, and delivers in 1.8 seconds or less. Develops the short passing game used against pressure.

name: Drop-Back Mechanics
desc: QBs work 3-step, 5-step, and 7-step drops without receivers. Focus on footwork, shoulder turn, and platform readiness at the top of each drop. Filmed for individual review.

name: Pocket Movement Drill
desc: QB works pocket movement — climb the pocket, slide left/right — while reading a downfield target held by a coach. Develops the ability to throw accurately while moving within structure.

name: Bootleg & Rollout Package
desc: QB executes play-action boots and sprint-outs to both sides. Receivers run defined rollout routes. Focus on QB hitching up his momentum before throwing across his body.

name: Mesh Concept Install
desc: Installs the mesh crossing concept — two shallow crossers rubbing at the mesh point with a wheel route behind. QB reads underneath to wheel based on linebacker drops.

name: Four Verticals
desc: All four receivers run vertical routes. QB reads the coverage and targets the open seam — inside releases vs Cover 2, outside vs Cover 3. Stretches the defense vertically.

name: Scramble Drill
desc: QB drops back and a coach signals scramble. Receivers break off their routes to find open grass while QB extends the play. Develops improvisation and off-schedule football.

name: Red Zone Pass Concepts
desc: Run compressed route combinations inside the 10 — fades, corners, back-of-the-end-zone digs. Defense plays tight man. Focus on back-shoulder throws and high-point catches.

name: WR Release vs Press
desc: WRs work against press coverage — stem, jab step, and release technique. Outside release, inside release, and speed release practiced one-on-one against a corner.

name: Air Raid Mesh & Spot
desc: Run the air-raid staples — mesh, stick, and Y-cross — against a scout defense at tempo. Ball is out in 2.5 seconds or less on every rep.

name: RPO Installation
desc: QB reads a pre-snap key and post-snap gives or pulls based on that read. Bubble screen, slant, and quick out attached to inside zone. Develops dual-threat QB instincts.

name: Inside Zone Install
desc: Offense installs inside zone with full O-line. RB reads the backside guard, finds the cutback or keeps the playside crease. Emphasis on combo blocks working to the second level.

name: Outside Zone / Stretch
desc: O-line and RB run the stretch play to both sides. RB aims for the outside hip of the TE and cuts back across the grain when the corner closes. Develops vision and patience.

name: Power Run Install
desc: Installs power — FB or TE kicks out the EMOL while the backside guard pulls and leads through the hole. RB runs off the lead block. Fundamental gap-scheme introduction.

name: Counter Trey
desc: Guard and tackle pull backside while the RB takes a false-step to freeze linebackers. Develops misdirection timing between the handoff and the RB's counter step.

name: Draw Play
desc: QB fakes a pass drop, hands to RB running a draw path. O-line sets pass protection then releases to second level. Develops deception timing and patience from the RB.

name: QB Keeper & Designed Run
desc: Mobile QB runs designed keepers, sneaks, and zone-read pulls. Focus on cut decision, shoulder pad lowering at contact, and protecting the football through the tackle.

name: RB Vision Cone Drill
desc: RB runs through cones simulating an O-line block sequence, reading a cut lane and hitting it at full speed. Develops patience behind the line and burst through the hole.

name: Stiff Arm & Jump Cut
desc: RB runs through a course with defenders positioned to tackle. Work the stiff arm, jump cut, and spin move in succession. Develops elusiveness after contact.

name: Toss Sweep
desc: QB tosses the ball to an RB sweeping the edge. WR cracks inside, FB kicks out the corner. Timing of the toss, arc of the RB, and WR crack timing all practiced in sequence.

name: Goal Line Run Package
desc: Run goal line formations from the 1- and 2-yard lines — QB sneak, fullback dive, off-tackle power. Physical reps with emphasis on low pad level and finishing through contact.

name: Screen Game Install
desc: Installs RB screen, middle screen, and tunnel screen. QB drops deep and throws back across the LOS. O-line releases to set blocks. RB reads the blocks downfield.

name: Zone Read Option
desc: QB reads the unblocked defensive end — if he crashes give to RB, if he stays pull and keep. Live reps with a scout DE making a decision. Develops QB mesh-point patience.

name: Hands Catching Technique
desc: Receivers catch 100 balls thrown to all catch windows — high, low, left, right, behind the head. Focus on soft hands, eyes tracking the ball to the tuck, and no body-catching.

name: Catch & Run After Catch
desc: WRs catch a pass then immediately work run-after-catch — turn upfield, avoid cones simulating defenders, finish to the end zone. Develops YAC instincts.

name: Contested Catch Drills
desc: WR and CB compete for 50/50 balls thrown to the boundary and end zone corners. WR works high-point, body positioning, and physicality through the catch.

name: Slant Route Timing
desc: WRs run slant routes at precise depths while QB drops three steps and fires on the break. Timing is everything — no double-catches.

name: Comeback & Out Route Precision
desc: Receivers run comebacks (14-16 yards) and out routes (6-8 yards) with emphasis on a clean break — no drift, sharp cut. QB delivers before the receiver comes out of his break.

name: TE Seam Route
desc: Tight ends run seam routes down the hash, working to split the safeties in Cover 2 or beat a linebacker over the top in Cover 1. Focus on releasing off the line and accelerating through.

name: RB Check-Down & Wheel Route
desc: RBs work the check-down catch in the flat and the wheel route — releasing out of the backfield, running the flat, then bending upfield. Timing coordinated with the QB.

name: Boundary Fade Route
desc: WR and QB work the boundary fade — WR presses inside, fades to the back pylon, QB lofts the ball to the back shoulder. Critical red-zone connection requiring precise ball placement.

name: Catch in Traffic
desc: Receiver runs a crossing route through a zone of defenders with pool noodles or bags. Must catch the ball cleanly while absorbing simulated contact mid-route. Builds courage over the middle.

name: Dig & Post Corner Route
desc: Receivers work the dig (in route at 12-15 yards) and post-corner combination. QB reads the safety — if safety drives on the post, throw the corner behind him.

name: WR Blocking Technique
desc: Receivers work crack blocks, stalk blocks on corners, and downfield seal blocks on linebackers. Perimeter blocking is often the difference in the run game.

name: Stance & First Step
desc: O-linemen work stance fundamentals — feet width, weight in balls of feet, flat back — then fire out on snap count with a 6-inch first step. Timed from snap to contact.

name: Hand Placement & Punch
desc: Linemen work hand placement on shield bags — thumbs up, inside the frame, elbows in — delivering a powerful punch that locks out the defender. Focus on timing and extension.

name: Double-Team Block
desc: Two linemen combo block a single defender — one takes the post, one takes the lead — working together to drive the defender off the ball, then one releases to the second level.

name: Pass Protection Mirror
desc: O-linemen set pass protection against a live rusher using mirror technique — kick-slide, maintain inside leverage, punch on contact. Focus on footwork and patience.

name: Pull Technique
desc: Guards and tackles work pull mechanics — open pivot step, flat pull path down the line, kick-out block on the EMOL or log block into the hole. Timed and filmed for review.

name: Zone Blocking Steps
desc: Full O-line practices zone blocking footwork — lateral first step to the playside, combo block with adjacent lineman, and climb to the linebacker. Run both inside and outside zone.

name: Reach Block
desc: Linemen practice reach blocks — getting outside leverage on a defender who is head-up or shaded away. Emphasis on the flat lateral first step and winning the hand fight outside.

name: Pass Set vs Speed Rush
desc: O-linemen face a speed-rush defensive end. Work the quick kick-slide, redirect laterally, and mirror the edge rusher without giving ground. Develops lateral pass-set quickness.

name: Chute & Sled Drive
desc: Linemen fire out through a blocking chute (forces low pad level) then drive a 7-man sled for 5 yards. Reinforces staying low and maintaining leg drive through the point of attack.

name: 5-Man Unit Communication
desc: O-line calls out protections together against various defensive alignments — 4-3, 3-4, nickel, overload blitz. Builds cohesion and pre-snap communication habits.

name: Get-Off & First Step Explosion
desc: D-linemen work off the line on a ball snap, driving through a bag on their first step. Emphasis on anticipating the snap count and getting off the ball as fast as legally possible.

name: Rip & Swim Pass Rush
desc: D-linemen work rip (under the arm) and swim (over the arm) pass-rush moves against a partner or shield bag. Emphasis on timing the move off the blocker's punch.

name: Bull Rush & Anchor
desc: Defensive tackles and ends bull rush a 2-man sled for 5 yards — drive through contact, maintain leverage, and anchor at the point of attack. Builds base strength and leg drive.

name: Counter Spin Move
desc: D-lineman sets up an outside rush, then counters with an inside spin move when the blocker oversets. Work the full sequence — setup, spin, burst to the QB — at game speed.

name: Run Fits & Gap Responsibility
desc: D-line works their gap assignments against a run-heavy scout offense. Each lineman is responsible for their gap and must not allow cutback by over-pursuing.

name: Tackle & Wrap Technique
desc: D-linemen practice open-field tackling — break down, eyes up, drive through the ball carrier with both arms wrapping, and drive the feet through contact. Finish every rep.

name: 3-Move Pass Rush Plan
desc: Each D-lineman works a 3-move pass-rush plan — primary move, counter, and counter to the counter. Partner resistance at half-speed to perfect technique before live reps.

name: Slant & Stunt Execution
desc: D-line runs T-T games and E-T stunts against a live O-line — one lineman occupies two blockers while the other crashes the gap. Timing and communication are critical.

name: Setting the Edge
desc: Defensive ends practice setting the edge against outside zone and toss runs — maintain outside leverage, squeeze the blocker inside, force the ball carrier to cut back to pursuit.

name: Block Destruction
desc: D-linemen practice defeating specific blocks — double teams, chip blocks from TEs, and reach blocks from guards. Work shedding each block type and redirecting to the ball.

name: Drop & Zone Coverage
desc: Linebackers drop into hook-curl, flat, and hook-to-curl zones on the snap. Read QB eyes, break on the ball's flight, and locate the receiver before making the tackle.

name: Man Coverage on RB & TE
desc: LBs cover RBs and TEs in man coverage out of the backfield and off the line. Work the release — bump and redirect at the LOS — then turn and run with the receiver.

name: Run-Pass Conflict Read
desc: LBs read the offensive tackle's block to determine run or pass. If tackle blocks down, flow to the run. If tackle sets pass protection, drop into coverage.

name: Blitz Package Install
desc: Install a variety of blitz packages — A-gap pressure, weak-side edge blitz, and zone blitz. LBs learn their assignment, timing off the snap, and how to convert if blocked.

name: Scrape Exchange
desc: DE crashes inside while LB scrapes around the edge on zone-read plays. Timing of the scrape, exchange of gap responsibilities, and proper LB path to the corner are emphasized.

name: Defeat the Block
desc: LBs work shedding offensive linemen attacking them in the second level — rip through, swim over, and press-and-shed techniques practiced against live blockers at half speed.

name: Angle Tackle Drill
desc: LBs practice angle tackling — approach from the side, break down at 2 yards, drive shoulder through the near hip, wrap arms and finish. Critical for open-field stops.

name: Fast Flow Pursuit
desc: LBs read the flow of the play and pursue to the football at full speed across multiple run scenarios — inside zone, outside zone, counter. Take correct pursuit angles to the ball.

name: Screen Recognition
desc: LBs read the screen — offensive linemen releasing, QB setting up deep — and immediately redirect to the flat to disrupt the screen at or behind the line of scrimmage.

name: Stack & Fill Technique
desc: ILBs work staying clean behind the D-line by stacking blocks, then filling the correct gap once the running lane is defined. Patience and instincts developed together.

name: Backpedal & Break Technique
desc: DBs work their backpedal — high hips, heel-to-toe roll, shoulders back — then break at 45 degrees on a coach's signal. Fundamental coverage skill drilled in isolation.

name: Press Coverage Technique
desc: CBs work press alignment — inside shade, punch the receiver's release point, and redirect inside or force outside before turning to run. Paired one-on-one with WRs at the line.

name: Zone Drop & Find the Ball
desc: DBs drop into their zone assignments, keep eyes on the QB through their drop, and break on the throw. Work all three levels — flat, hook, and deep third.

name: Bail Technique (Cover 3 CB)
desc: Boundary corner bails at the snap — opens hips, gains depth, and flies to the deep third. Work against receivers running double moves; CB must not bite on the first cut.

name: Safety Run Fill
desc: Safeties rotate down on run support — read the block of the TE or H-back, fill the edge gap, and make the open-field tackle. Emphasis on decisive trigger and safe approach angle.

name: Half-Field Coverage Drill
desc: Strong safety and corner work half-field coverage together — corner takes deep half, safety plays the flat-to-curl. Receivers run combination routes to stress the coverage.

name: Pick-Six Intercept Drill
desc: DBs track underthrown or batted balls — turn and run with the ball in the air, catch at the highest point, and score. Reinforces return instincts after the interception.

name: Trail Technique in Man
desc: DB trails the receiver at hip level in man coverage — inside leverage, one arm's length, eyes on the receiver's numbers until the ball is in the air, then find and play the ball.

name: Safety Communication
desc: Free and strong safety align together and communicate coverage calls, rotation checks, and alert calls before and at the snap. Builds the communication habit essential at every level.

name: Bump & Run Gauntlet
desc: CB runs the gauntlet — press the first receiver, release, sprint to the next WR, bump, and repeat. Builds the explosive change-of-direction needed to maintain press coverage.

name: Post Safety Help
desc: Free safety works the post rotation — reads the post route threat, rotates over the top, and arrives at the deep middle hash in time to contest the catch or make the tackle.

name: Kickoff Coverage Lanes
desc: 11-man kickoff unit practices lane discipline — each player maintains their lane, avoids the wedge, and converges on the returner. Film to check containment and lane integrity.

name: Kickoff Return Blocks
desc: Return team installs the kickoff return scheme — wedge formation, up-man assignments, and the returner's cut lane. Work both middle and sideline return calls.

name: Punt Coverage
desc: Punt coverage team works their release off the line — gunners beat jams, personal protectors support, and coverage converges on the returner without over-running the ball.

name: Punt Return Scheme
desc: Install the punt return — fair-catch priority, wall return left/right, or safe return middle. Returner catches punts under pressure while the front 8 set the return wall.

name: PAT & Field Goal Protection
desc: Kicking team installs PAT and field goal protection — line splits, personal protector depth, and snapper-holder-kicker timing. Work at full speed with a scout rush.

name: Field Goal Block Package
desc: Defense installs a field goal block package — penetration lanes, timing off the snap, and hand-up technique at the kick point. One block changes a game.

name: Onside Kick Execution
desc: Kicking team practices the onside kick — directional kick, recovery alignment, and execution timing. Also install the hands team on the receiving end.

name: Fake Punt Install
desc: Install one or two fake punt plays — direct snap to an up-back, punter pass to the gunner, or spread punt with a designed run. Run against a full punt return team.

name: Snapper Accuracy
desc: Long and short snapper work isolated reps — 15-yard snap to punter, 7-yard snap to holder. Timed and filmed. Work spiral rotation and target accuracy.

name: Gunner Release Drill
desc: Punt gunners work releasing off a jammer at the line — speed release, inside release, and double-move release. Get off the line fast and run to the ball.

name: Coffin Corner Punt
desc: Punter works directional kicking targeting the coffin corner inside the 10-yard line. Focus on hang time, directional accuracy, and making the returner field under pressure.

name: Inside-the-10 Pass Concepts
desc: Run compressed route combinations inside the 10 — fade-stop, corner-flat, back-shoulder throw. Field is shortened; every route must be tighter and more precise.

name: Goal Line Run Package
desc: Install goal line formations — QB sneak, jumbo package power, off-tackle, and naked bootleg. Physical reps with full line contact. Emphasis on winning the leverage battle.

name: Red Zone Defense Cover 0
desc: Defense plays Cover 0 man with no free safety. DBs must cover with no help over the top. LBs match any eligible receiver out of the backfield. High-intensity competitive reps.

name: Red Zone 7-on-7
desc: Offense runs red zone pass concepts vs. defense's red zone coverage shells — alternating Cover 2, Cover 0, and zone blitz. Score or stop is the only outcome.

name: 2-Point Conversion Package
desc: Install 2-point conversion plays from the 3-yard line — quick slant, QB sneak, designed run, and play-action fade. Defense plays live. Outcome tracked for game planning.

name: 4th-Down Package
desc: Install the 4th-and-short and 4th-and-goal package — power, QB sneak, and a short pass concept. Offense and defense compete live. Builds clutch execution habits.

name: Red Zone Run Fits
desc: Defense works gap assignments and run fits inside the 20 where field space is compressed. Linebackers must fit quickly with no pursuit angle available.

name: Red Zone Situational Script
desc: Run a full scripted red zone period — 1st-and-goal, 2nd-and-goal, 3rd-and-short, 4th-and-goal. Both units compete with downs and distance tracked. Film and review.

name: Two-Minute Offense Install
desc: Install the two-minute offense — no-huddle operation, spike play, sideline routes, and clock management. QB must communicate at the line of scrimmage and get the ball snapped with urgency.

name: Two-Minute Defense
desc: Defense practices the two-minute drill — zone prevent, don't give up the sideline, allow only short completions. Tackling in bounds is the priority. Clock management is everything.

name: Clock Management Scenarios
desc: Offense and defense work specific clock scenarios — 90 seconds no timeouts, 45 seconds two timeouts, final spike play. Make decisions in real time under simulated pressure.

name: Hurry-Up No-Huddle
desc: Offense operates the full hurry-up no-huddle at game speed — signal system from the sideline, alignment adjustments at the line, and quick snap execution.

name: Spike Play Execution
desc: QB and offense practice the spike play — fast break from the previous play, get set, and snap-and-spike in under 3 seconds. Timing between the QB and offensive line is critical.

name: Final Drive Simulation
desc: Simulate a full final drive — offense starts at own 25 with 1:45 and one timeout, needs a field goal to win. Defense plays live. Tracks real time, real downs, real decisions.

name: 11-on-11 Full Scrimmage
desc: Full team scrimmage with live contact. Run from a full script — run and pass plays on offense, base defense with all fronts and coverages. Film every series for review.

name: Thud Scrimmage
desc: Full team live reps at thud contact — full speed to the tackle, contact is controlled (no takedowns). Reduces injury risk while maintaining competitive execution at practice speed.

name: Situational Scrimmage
desc: Scripted situation scrimmage — 3rd-and-short, 3rd-and-long, red zone, two-minute — run in sequence. Both units compete for results. Builds situational awareness and execution.

name: Scout Team vs. Starters
desc: Scout team runs the opponent's base offense and defense against the starting units. Prepares the team for specific tendencies, formations, and plays they will see on game day.

name: Game Plan Install Period
desc: Walk through the week's game plan at full speed. Every play installed against live opposition. Focus is on execution of the new install, not winning competitive reps.

name: Turnover Circuit Scrimmage
desc: Defense earns extra plays for turnovers — every pick or fumble recovery earns a fresh drive. Rewards defensive playmaking and instills a turnover mindset on both sides of the ball.

name: 110s (Gassers)
desc: Players sprint 110 yards (sideline to sideline and back), rest 30 seconds, repeat 6-10 times. Builds lactate threshold and mental toughness.

name: Position 40-Yard Dash Sets
desc: Players run position-specific 40-yard dashes with timed rest between reps. Linemen run every 90 seconds; skill players every 60 seconds. Tracked and compared weekly.

name: Pursuit Drill
desc: All 11 defensive players sprint to the ball from their assigned positions on a play call — even if the ball is on the opposite side. Develops pursuit habit and full-team conditioning.

name: Oklahoma Drill
desc: One-on-one — blocker and defender in a confined lane with a ball carrier. Physical, competitive reps that test toughness, leverage, and contact conditioning. Rotate all positions.

name: Up-Downs
desc: Players jog in place then drop to the ground and jump back up as fast as possible on coach's signal. Work in 30-second intervals. Develops full-body explosiveness and conditioning.

name: Bear Crawl & Crab Walk
desc: Players bear crawl 10 yards and crab walk back for multiple rounds. Develops functional core strength, shoulder stability, and conditioning that translates to low-pad-level play.

name: Metabolic Agility Circuit
desc: Four-station circuit: sprint 20 yards, 10 push-ups, lateral shuffle 10 yards each way, and 5 squat jumps. 45 seconds on, 15 off for three rounds. Full-body metabolic conditioning.

name: Position Conditioning Finish
desc: End practice with position-specific conditioning — linemen run 10-yard short sprints; skill players run routes for conditioning. Everyone finishes together as a team.

name: Mat Drills
desc: Team works a sequence of mat drills — barrel rolls, forward-backward rolls, and fireman carries. Builds full-body strength, coordination, and mental toughness through fatigue.

name: Sprint-to-Jog Intervals
desc: Players alternate 20-yard sprints with 20-yard jogs for 8-12 intervals. Mimics the work-rest ratio of football plays — explosive effort followed by brief recovery before going again.

name: Captain's Huddle
desc: Team captains lead a brief meeting at midfield — address the team on effort, culture, and the week's theme. Builds leadership habits and gives captains a platform before the team.

name: Compete Card Recognition
desc: Coaches recognize players who earned compete cards during the week for exceptional effort or improvement. Public recognition in front of the team builds a culture of accountability.

name: Unit Challenge Competition
desc: Offense vs. defense in a non-football challenge — tug-of-war, relay race, or trivia. Builds team cohesion and competitive spirit in a fun setting outside of scheme.

name: Film Room Accountability
desc: Team reviews the best and worst execution from the previous game on a projected screen on the field. Accountability is public and immediate — builds a film culture across the roster.

name: Post-Practice Bring-It-In
desc: Full team gathers at midfield after practice. Captains lead the break — a word, a chant, or a commitment. Ends every practice with unity and sets the tone for the next session.

name: Water Break
desc: Scheduled hydration break with players at their position group stations. Coaches use this time to give brief individual corrections while players hydrate and recover between periods.

name: Equipment Check Break
desc: Players check and adjust pads, helmets, and cleats. Trainers circulate to address minor tape jobs or equipment issues before returning to full-speed practice.

name: Transition Between Periods
desc: Planned transition time between major practice periods. Coaches set up the next drill while players move to their next formation. Builds efficient practice habits.
